L1.area: Database driver layer and connection handling logic

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied data used to construct a database sequence name is interpolated directly into a SQL query without proper escaping, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary SQL statements when the application connects to a PostgreSQL database.

L3.area: PostgreSQL database driver layer and its record ID retrieval logic
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied table and field name parameters used to construct a database sequence name are interpolated directly into a SQL query without proper escaping, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary SQL statements when the application connects to a PostgreSQL database and retrieves the last inserted record ID.

ground_truth.reason: The pg_insert_id() method directly concatenates user-supplied table and field name parameters into a SQL query without proper escaping via pg_escape_identifier(), enabling SQL injection.
